HW8_NLP_Serino

Clean Text

nicole_genie <- function(data, text_col, lemmatize = TRUE, omit_stops = TRUE, 
                         local_path = "/Users/tut38747/Desktop") {
  
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(stringi)
  library(tibble)
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Check inputs
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  # Check if data is a dataframe
  if (!is.data.frame(data)) stop("Input must be a dataframe!")
  
  # Check if text_col exists
  if (!text_col %in% names(data)) stop(paste0("Column '", text_col, "' not found!"))
  
  # Combine all text into one string
  x <- paste(data[[text_col]], collapse = " ")
  
  if (!is.character(x)) stop("Text column must contain character data!")
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Load cleaning data
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  # Loading stopword lists
  stops_file <- file.path(local_path, "Temple_stops25.rda")
  replacements_file <- file.path(local_path, "replacements_25.rda")
  
  load(stops_file)
  load(replacements_file)
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Text cleaning 1
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  # Make all lowercase
  x <- tolower(x)
  
  # Standardize apostrophes
  x <- stringi::stri_replace_all_regex(
    x,
    "[\u2018\u2019\u02BC\u201B\uFF07\u0092\u0091\u0060\u00B4\u2032\u2035]",
    "'"
  )
  
  # Remove all number digits from text
  x <- gsub("[[:digit:]]", " ", x)
  
  # Remove singleton characters (single letters surrounded by spaces)
  x <- gsub("\\s.\\s", " ", x)
  
  # Remove extra spaces that occur more than once in a row
  x <- gsub("\\s+", " ", x)
  
  # Trim leading/trailing whitespace
  x <- trimws(x)
  
  # Split the text into individual words and keep as a tibble
  split <- tibble(word = unlist(strsplit(x, " ")))
  
  # Remove empty strings
  split <- split %>% filter(word != "")
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Replace patterns
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  split$word <- stringi::stri_replace_all_fixed(
    split$word,
    pattern = replacements_25$word,
    replacement = replacements_25$replacement,
    vectorize_all = FALSE
  )
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Remove Stop Words
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  if (omit_stops) {
    Temple_stops25_vec <- as.character(Temple_stops25$word)
    split <- split %>% filter(!word %in% Temple_stops25_vec)
  }
  
  # Remove empty strings that may have been left
  split <- split %>% filter(word != "")
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Split again
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  split2 <- split %>%
    mutate(word = strsplit(word, " ")) %>%
    unnest(word)
  
  split2 <- split2 %>%
    mutate(word = trimws(word)) %>%
    filter(word != "")
  
  # Remove punctuation
  split2 <- split2 %>%
    mutate(word = gsub("[[:punct:]]", "", word))
  
  split2 <- split2 %>% filter(word != "")
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Lemmatize
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  if (lemmatize) {
    split2 <- split2 %>%
      mutate(word = textstem::lemmatize_words(word))
  }
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Clean
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  # Remove digits that may have been revealed by replacements
  split2$word <- gsub("[[:digit:]]", " ", split2$word)
  
  # Remove singleton characters again
  split2$word <- gsub("\\s.\\s", " ", split2$word)
  
  # Remove extra spaces again
  split2$word <- gsub("\\s+", " ", split2$word)
  
  # Trim whitespace
  split2 <- split2 %>% mutate(word = trimws(word))
  
  # Remove any remaining empty strings or NA values
  split2 <- split2 %>%
    filter(word != "" & !is.na(word))
  
  # Remove abbreviations and short words
  split2 <- split2 %>%
    filter(nchar(word) > 2)
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Remove Roman numerals
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  split2 <- split2 %>%
    filter(!grepl("^[ivxlcdm]+$", word))
  
  # ________________________________________________________________
  # Return cleaned words
  # ________________________________________________________________
  
  return(split2$word) 
  
}

Construct a dataframe with 1000 random word pairs

library(SemanticDistance)
library(tidytext)
library(janeaustenr)
library(dplyr)

# Get Jane Austen corpus 
austen_df <- austen_books()

# Clean
word_list <- nicole_genie(
  data = austen_df,
  text_col = "text",
  lemmatize = TRUE,
  omit_stops = TRUE,
  local_path = "/Users/tut38747/Desktop"
)
# Create 1000 random pairs
set.seed(42)
df <- data.frame(
  word1 = sample(word_list, 1000, replace = TRUE),
  word2 = sample(word_list, 1000, replace = TRUE)
)
print(head(df, 50))
##             word1             word2
## 1            pass           suppose
## 2          justly               not
## 3            find              word
## 4            girl           ability
## 5            only              just
## 6            make              body
## 7      charitable           distant

## 9   insupportable         splendour
## 10 unapproachable              have
## 11            not           gravity
## 12            not          jennings
## 13            but               and
## 14        perfect              miss
## 15        eagerly              pass
## 16           else furnishedpleasant
## 17        scruple          treasure
## 18          heris              ever
## 19           over              good
## 20         people              only
## 21           much         principal
## 22      artlessly              shew
## 23         endure              plan
## 24        forster              meet
## 25           hope               not
## 26     neverbless            engage
## 27         listen               and
## 28           have              town
## 29        ashamed             equal
## 30        confess            spirit
## 31         relish        shoestring
## 32            out              give
## 33           much           support
## 34     singularly               not
## 35           only           country
## 36          where              when
## 37   insufferable             there
## 38        climate          helpless
## 39           dear              hear
## 40        however            falter
## 41         unwell              just
## 42   inconvenient           morland
## 43          dream             stair
## 44           very              wife
## 45          charm             darcy
## 46            say              even
## 47          delay          surprise
## 48      neighbour           receive
## 49           miss           feeling
## 50         advise             marry

Use the SemanticDistance package to clean and generate cosine distance norms

# Clean
WordPairs_Clean <- clean_paired_cols(dat=df, wordcol1='word1', wordcol2='word2', lemmatize=TRUE)
knitr::kable(head(WordPairs_Clean, 6), format = "simple", digits=2)
id_row_orig word1_clean word2_clean word1 word2
1 pass suppose pass suppose
2 justly not justly not
3 find word find word
4 girl ability girl ability
5 only just only just
6 make body make body 
# Calc Distance
Columns_Dists <- dist_paired_cols(WordPairs_Clean)
knitr::kable(head(Columns_Dists, 6), format = "pipe", digits = 2)
id_row_orig word1_clean word2_clean word1 word2 CosDist_SD15 CosDist_GLO
1 pass suppose pass suppose 1.29 0.59
2 justly not justly not 0.62 0.79
3 find word find word 1.25 0.38
4 girl ability girl ability 0.75 0.70
5 only just only just 0.29 0.09
6 make body make body 0.29 0.42

Interpolate Missing Values

library(zoo)

# Interpolate missing values
Columns_Dists$CosDist_SD15 <- zoo::na.approx(Columns_Dists$CosDist_SD15, na.rm=FALSE)
Columns_Dists$CosDist_GLO  <- zoo::na.approx(Columns_Dists$CosDist_GLO,  na.rm=FALSE)
head(Columns_Dists, n=20)
id_row_orig word1_clean word2_clean word1 word2 CosDist_SD15 CosDist_GLO
1 pass suppose pass suppose 1.29 0.59
2 justly not justly not 0.62 0.79
3 find word find word 1.25 0.38
4 girl ability girl ability 0.75 0.70
5 only just only just 0.29 0.09
6 make body make body 0.29 0.42
7 charitable distant charitable distant 1.26 0.95
8 involve other involve other 0.89 0.36
9 insupportable splendour insupportable splendour 1.29 0.93
10 unapproachable have unapproachable have 1.69 0.99
11 not gravity not gravity 0.89 0.67
12 not jennings not jennings 0.63 0.37
13 but and but and 0.37 0.07
14 perfect miss perfect miss 1.19 0.55
15 eagerly pass eagerly pass 1.06 0.84
16 else furnishedpleasant else furnishedpleasant 1.40 0.95
17 scruple treasure scruple treasure 1.74 1.06
18 heris ever heris ever 1.08 0.67
19 over good over good 0.42 0.27
20 people only people only 1.66 0.14

Visualize Distributions

# Distribution of Embedding
ggplot(Columns_Dists, aes(x=CosDist_SD15)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=50, fill='lightblue', color='white') +
  labs(title="SD15 Cosine Distances",
       x="Cosine Distance", y="Count") +
  jamie.theme

# Distribution of Experiential
ggplot(Columns_Dists, aes(x=CosDist_GLO)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=50, fill='yellow', color='white') +
  labs(title="GloVe Cosine Distances",
       x="Cosine Distance", y="Count") +
  jamie.theme

Compare Experiential vs Embedding

ggplot(Columns_Dists, aes(x=CosDist_SD15, y=CosDist_GLO)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method='lm') +
  labs(title="Experiential vs Embedding Cosine Distances",
       x="SD15", y="GloVe") +
  jamie.theme

cor.test(Columns_Dists$CosDist_SD15, Columns_Dists$CosDist_GLO,
         method='pearson', use='complete.obs')
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  Columns_Dists$CosDist_SD15 and Columns_Dists$CosDist_GLO
## t = 5.2831, df = 998, p-value = 1.56e-07
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.1040129 0.2246386
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.1649425

What does this all mean????? Interpertation.

The histograms show differences between the two measuments.The wider SD15 distribution shows it captures greater variability in semantic relationships, while GloVe’s tighter distribution reflects more consistent text-based co-occurrence patterns.

The correlation between GloVe and SD15 distances was very weak (r = 0.18, p < 0.001)). GloVe captures text-based patterns while SD15 emphasizes perceptual features—for Jane Austen’s literary vocabulary, these yield different distance estimates.
